files.rowids_format: uint32_t[row_count] grouped by adsh_code in input row order

storage_extensions.usage: mmap offsets.bin and rowids.bin; iterate only the `num` rowid slices for FY-2023 filing codes, then aggregate each filing's surviving `USD` non-null rows by `(tag,version)` before joining to the filtered `pre` slice

join_order.notes: Apply the selective filing filter first and drive all downstream work only from FY-2023 filing codes.

join_order.build: tiny per-filing hash_agg(tag,version -> sum(value), cnt) from qualifying filing num rows after uom='USD' and value IS NOT NULL
join_order.probe: qualified pre(stmt='IS') rows for the same filing
join_order.notes: Aggregate duplicate num facts before the pre join, then scan each filing's short pre slice once and look up matching `(tag,version)` pairs.

data_structures.fact_join: flat_array(pre_is_offsets_by_adsh) + compact SoA payload arrays for qualifying stmt='IS' rows + tiny per-filing hash_agg keyed by (tag,version) storing sum,cnt

optimization_notes: Root cause: the iter_4 filing-centric plan still performs Category A/C work proportional to raw joined fact rows, probing the per-filing pre structure and updating the final aggregate once for each of the 10,612,661 surviving `USD` non-null `num` rows even though those rows collapse to 2,301,934 distinct `(tag,version)` pairs within qualifying filings and only 491,722 pairs actually match filtered `pre` rows. The revised plan keeps the successful `sub.fy=2023` first filter and the compact `pre(stmt='IS')` payloads by filing, but changes the within-filing join strategy to aggregate `num` by `(tag,version)` before joining: build a tiny cache-resident per-filing hash aggregate over the filing's `num` slice, then scan the filing's short `pre` slice once and look up each `(tag,version)` in that map before updating the final `(name,tag,plabel)` aggregate. This eliminates repeated join probes and redundant final aggregate updates while keeping the hot per-filing working set in L1/L2.
